title: goodenia scapigera subsp. graniticola
intro: Goodenia scapigera subsp. graniticola, also known as the granite Goodenia, is a rare and endemic plant species native to Western Australia. The leaves and flowers of this plant are traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Aboriginal cultures, where it is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Goodenia scapigera subsp. graniticola make it beneficial for treating a variety of health issues, including wounds, skin infections, and digestive problems. It can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or dried plant material, though its use should be done under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
